channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: TTAP Holds Protest Rally Over 27th Constitutional Amendment, Announces Nationwide Black Day
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The Tehreek Tahaffuz-e-Ayeen-Pakistan (TTAP) organised a significant protest rally on Tuesday against the 27th Constitutional Amendment, marching from Parliament House to the Supreme Court....

item.title: Uttar Pradesh Seer Arrested Over Alleged Illegal Religious Conversion Network
item.description: In Balrampur district's Rehra Maafi village near Utraula town, residents still recall Jamaluddin, now widely known as Chhangur Baba. Once a modest trader cycling between villages and selling trinkets,...

item.title: How AI, Robotics, and Wearables Are Shaping Workplace Safety in Canada
item.description: Around 60 per cent of Canadian employees are expected to see their jobs transformed by artificial intelligence (AI), mostly as a complement rather than a replacement. AI technologies—including wearabl...

item.title: Canada Launches Carbon Removal Coalition to Secure $100 Million in Investments by 2030
item.description: Canada’s emerging carbon removal sector is uniting under a new initiative, the Advance Carbon Removal Coalition, aiming to attract $100 million in project investments by 2030. The coalition brings tog...

item.title: Analysing the Green Party's national polling surge after their Gorton and Denton byelection victory
item.description: A recent YouGov poll conducted after the Green Party's historic byelection win in Gorton and Denton shows the party reaching 21% national support, overtaking Labour (which has fallen behind) and closi...

item.title: Ofwat fines South East Water £22m for repeated service failures affecting customers in Kent and Sussex
item.description: Ofwat has announced that South East Water will face a £22 million fine due to repeated failures in water supply across Kent and Sussex between 2020 and 2023, which disrupted service for over 286,000 p...

item.title: How Elephant Migration Guides Human-Wildlife Coexistence in Northern Kenya
item.description: In Northern Kenya, the movement of elephants like the 24-year-old matriarch Liana highlights how humans and wildlife share landscapes under climate stress. In January 2026, GPS collars tracked Liana a...

item.title: MEA refutes claims of US using Indian ports amid Middle East conflict
item.description: The Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) of India has strongly refuted claims that the United States is using Indian ports to launch attacks on Iran. The allegations surfaced following an interview by f...

item.title: The Benefits and Experiences of Guided Cycling Tours Abroad
item.description: Guided cycling adventures abroad offer a slower, more immersive way to explore destinations compared to faster modes of travel like cars or buses. By pedaling at an unhurried pace, riders can notice i...

item.title: Daily Horoscopes and Astrological Guidance for March 5, 2026
item.description: The Globe and Mail presents the daily horoscope for March 5, 2026, offering astrological guidance tailored to each zodiac sign. Those celebrating their birthday today should take note of the sun-Jupit...

item.title: OpenAI Exploring Deployment of AI on NATO’s Unclassified Networks
item.description: OpenAI, the artificial intelligence company supported by Microsoft, Amazon, and others, is reportedly considering a contract to deploy its AI systems on NATO’s unclassified networks. This development ...

item.title: Brodie Kostecki takes pole position despite feeling unwell
item.description: Brodie Kostecki, the talented Supercars driver, experienced an unusual and challenging moment during the lead-up to the opening race of the weekend at Albert Park. While on his way to securing pole po...

item.title: Bandit Attack on Borgu Communities in Niger State Leaves 15 Dead
item.description: The death toll from a recent bandit attack on some communities in Borgu Local Government Area of Niger State has now reached 15 as of Sunday, March 1, 2026. The affected communities are Tashan Maje, S...

item.title: Tanzania Defends Its Ability to Handle Human Rights Issues Domestically, Cites Independent Probe into October 29 Incident
item.description: Tanzania's Permanent Representative to the UN in Geneva, Ambassador Abdallah Possi, has made it clear that the country is still strong and capable of handling its own matters, especially on human righ...

item.title: Nigeria and Niger Customs Commit to Improving Border Infrastructure and Trade
item.description: The Nigeria Customs Service (NCS) don talk say dem dey serious about improving border infrastructure and trade operations, especially with Niger Republic. Dis one come after a high-level meeting betwe...

item.title: US Administration Maintains Flexibility on Ground Forces Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: Washington is facing heightened tensions as the US, under the Trump administration, intensifies its military operations against Iran. White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt clarified that deploy...

item.title: How Gulf rivalries and diplomacy are shaping Kenya and Tanzania's foreign relations
item.description: Article inuambia kweli Kenya na Tanzania sasa ziko kati ya matumizi ya nguvu kati ya mifumo kwa kipato cha Kiarabu na viongozi kuu vya Ulaya. Kipindi cha kusadikiwa kwa uhalali, kushoto wa kisiasa, na...

item.title: Rahul Gandhi urges stronger response after Iranian warship sinks in Indian Ocean
item.description: On Thursday, Congress leader Rahul Gandhi criticised the Centre over escalating tensions in West Asia following the sinking of the Iranian warship IRIS Dena by a US submarine in the Indian Ocean. He w...

item.title: Kenya Integrates WHO Child Development Tool in 2025/26 National Health Survey
item.description: Kenya has made a major move in tracking the growth and development of its young children by including the World Health Organization's (WHO) child development tool in its national health survey for the...

item.title: Anthropic CEO Accuses OpenAI of Misleading Claims in Pentagon AI Contract Deal
item.description: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei sharply criticized OpenAI and its CEO Sam Altman in an internal memo, calling OpenAI's public statements about its new Department of Defense (DoD) contract 'straight up lies...
